title: woodsia subcordata
intro: Woodsia subcordata, also known as the cord fern or western woodsia, is a species of fern native to the western United States and Canada. It is a type of terrestrial fern with delicate fronds and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Woodsia subcordata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat skin irritations, making it a popular remedy in traditional herbal medicine. Woodsia subcordata can be prepared into teas, infusions, and salves, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of conditions, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
